title: Momentum in Iranian crude/condensate exports slows
section_path: Momentum in Iranian crude/condensate exports slows
section_path_text: Momentum in Iranian crude/condensate exports slows
summary:
This insight explores why Iran's crude/condensate export levels slowed from previous months and floating storage levels declined. By Armen Azizian
Iran's crude/condensate exports were observed at 1.3mbd in October, a 100kbd decline from the Q3 average and the lowest volume observed since May. This is still 300kbd higher than the 2022 average and is a 100kbd increase y-o-y, supporting Iran's elevated crude exports observed this year.
